pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors, fix issues with models

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
i have worries about this and scale.

if there are 1000 active sagas -- not really that much considering our scale goals -- then the expected value for time taken for all this is `500 milliseconds * 1000 = 500 seconds = 8.3 minutes`.

can we reduce this to `between(0, 100)` or even better `between(0, 10)`?

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Sure...also happy to just remove it altogether. I think anything here is an improvement in heartbeat behavior, as even without any delay we'd eventually get to a state where saga start times are well distributed.

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors, fix issues with models

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
it might make some sense to make this class implement `Startable` interface, `enabled = true if dispatcher`, and then the `onStart()` method would have `createActors()`. also opens up this class for doing other "saga actor monitoring" stuff if needed beyond just upon class instantiation.

not necessary for this PR if you dont want, but food for thought.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: on second thought, we might want this to be a `Periodic` -- see my other comment https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/40348/files#r1521150379

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Yeah I'm going to punt that until we have a use case for creating actors outside of pod restart

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors, fix issues with models

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: nit: it's not a heartbeat, is it? it's the "confirm saga actor exists"

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors, fix issues with models

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
im worried that if any transient exception happens during publishing the event and sleeping for any individual saga, we will never process any more events for any sagas that are "later" in the `for` statement above.

is that how this works?

if so we may need to do something like this:

- make this class a `Periodic`.
- catch errors for sagas individually instead of as a whole (i.e. try/catch inside the `for`)
- store the initial saga ids as a class variable with `var preExistingSagasThatNeedAnActor: Set[ObjectId] = sagaStateStorage.getNotCompletedSagas.ids` (i.e. make a set of all the saga ids -- I suggested some name like `preExistingSagasThatNeedAnActor` just to make it clear the job of this thing is to ensure actors are created for sagas that already existed when this `Periodic` was instantiated, since presumably any sagas created after that have their actor created by other means)
- the `onTick()` of this `Periodic` iterates `preExistingSagasThatNeedAnActor`, deleting the saga id from the set if publishing is successful
- the `Periodic` frequency could be a minute, or two, or whatever seems good. usually, it would do all it's work in the first call to `onTick()`, but ocaisionally there would be exceptions and those sagas would be handled in subsequent ticks, and then once everything is settled, the `onTick()` would be a no-op.

does this make sense?

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: I think this might be kinda overkill; our previous heartbeat generator had the exact same publishing behavior and the ability to publish heartbeats was not a source of issues for us. This class operates in the same way but with a greatly reduced scope, as any event that pertains to a saga will also trigger the saga actor to be created. As such this class now exists as a way to jumpstart saga heartbeat ticks rather than an absolute necessity for out state machine to work.

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Ok, I looked at it again and made a smaller change where I move the try-catch within the processing of each saga, so if publishing an event for a single saga fails we won't stop publishing all the other events.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
ok. i didnt realize any event would create the actor, i was thinking we *needed* this thing to work.

though, i think it's worth pointing out that the heartbeat generator was a periodic, so it's not the exact same publishing behavior. now, if on the first time there's a problem publishing a heartbeat for a saga, it just wont get heartbeats or an actor until some event happens for it. before, if there was a problem on the first time, the next `onTick()` would still happen a minute later.

so, im not sure. do you think it's still ok?

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: discussed in slack, we think it's ok -- we don't even strictly _need_ these heartbeats for the system to function, since any actual event related to the saga will create the actor move things along.

pr_title: DOM-54930 Move heartbeat generation to actors, fix issues with models

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
do you know how these `SagaEventProcessorActor` classes are destroyed/garbage collected after the saga is completed?

i can't quite grok how they are cleaned up -- will think a bit

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: They aren't explicitly cleaned up, I'm not sure if akka is smart enough to do anything behind the scenes. I considered trying to do some sort of cleanup, but was worried about increasing the risk of this PR when some of my early attempts caused errors. This PR doesn't change the number of actors created, so it shouldn't introduce any new issues with non cleaning things up worse

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
yea i agree you didnt introduce any new not-clean-up behavior here. i'm just surprised in our existing code it's not very clear if/how these things are cleaned up.

i agree we shouldn't do anything more in this PR. but also, i'll (or our team) should investigate if/how the cleanup happens. this seems like a potential memory leak. it's top of mind for me because i just got assigned a regression in dispatcher OOM during api test https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-55376

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body: Filed https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-55396

comments.body: shouldn't this be `{data_dir}/{subdirectory_name}`?

comments.author: ddl-eric-jin
comments.body: It should be `data_subdir/data_subdir` since I am uploading the same directory with the same file twice. I agree it is slightly confusing but I chose try to make it simpler with the same name name instead of renaming the folder before the second upload. But, I appreciate the attention to detail!

comments.author: ddl-eric-jin
comments.body: Also, for uploading things to a dataset via the CLI, it doesn't upload the containing folder and `{data_dir}` actually isn't uploaded.

pr_title: DOM-54805 - [UI] Add new Projects Billing tag search and bulk update table

comments.author: ddl-galias
comments.body:
Something to consider here, as you are awaiting fetch projects, you could see the tag deleted toast and then tag was not deleted one if this fails. 
In practice this scenario is imposible as we know the implementation of fetch projects can not throw an error, but I think that a component should not rely on knowing the implementation of a prop.
